items.headline: Tech-led Rally Drives S&P 500 and Nasdaq to New Highs
items.summary: U.S. equities closed an eventful week on a high note, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching new record highs on Friday, April 24, 2026. A standout earnings report from Intel fueled a surge in semiconductor and technology stocks, reinforcing a 'risk-on' market sentiment. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, saw a slight dip. Investors are also digesting news regarding the Federal Reserve's leadership transition and anticipating upcoming earnings reports from major tech companies.

items.headline: Intel Soars on Q1 Earnings Beat
items.summary: Intel Corporation ($INTC) stock surged significantly after reporting a 'stunning' first-quarter earnings beat, marking its best day since 1987. The chipmaker's performance fueled a broader rally in the semiconductor sector.

items.headline: Advanced Micro Devices Continues Ascent
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) also saw strong gains, contributing to the overall strength in technology and semiconductor stocks following positive market sentiment.

items.headline: Charter Communications Drops
items.summary: Charter Communications ($CHTR) experienced a notable decline, making it one of the worst-performing US-listed stocks for the week ending April 24, 2026.

items.headline: Medpace Holdings Under Pressure
items.summary: Medpace Holdings ($MEDP) also featured among the significant losers for the week, with its stock price falling substantially.

items.headline: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Paving Way for Warsh as Fed Chair
items.summary: The Department of Justice has closed its criminal investigation into current Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, a development that could smooth the path for Kevin Warsh's potential confirmation as the next Fed chair. The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is widely anticipated to maintain current interest rates at its upcoming April 28-29 meeting.

items.headline: Technology and Communication Services Lead Sector Performance
items.summary: Information Technology and Communication Services emerged as leading sectors for the month-to-date, driven by strong demand for AI and tech shares. Conversely, consumer and financial names, as well as healthcare, lagged the broader market. The Energy sector, while strong year-to-date, experienced a pullback in April.

items.headline: Meta and Microsoft Earnings on Deck Next Week
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting first-quarter earnings reports from tech giants Meta Platforms ($META) and Microsoft ($MSFT), both scheduled for Wednesday, April 29. These reports will be critical in assessing the monetization of significant AI investments. AT&T ($T) already reported strong Q1 results, with revenues up 2.9% and adjusted EPS of $0.57.

items.headline: Analysts Project Strong S&P 500 Gains for 2026 Amid Geopolitical Concerns
items.summary: Wall Street analysts anticipate the S&P 500 to advance by 11.8% in 2026, exceeding its 30-year average, propelled by expected robust earnings growth, corporate tax breaks, and substantial investments in AI infrastructure. The ongoing Iran conflict remains a macroeconomic factor, influencing oil prices and global economic sentiment.
